title: miliusa eupoda
intro: Miliusa eupoda is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae, native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia, commonly known as the Himalayan or Asian Miliusa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, leaves, and roots, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The seeds and roots of Miliusa eupoda contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been reported to have potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-cancer, and anti-diabetic effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed fresh or dried, or used as a topical application for skin and wound care.
